name: Montilla-Moriles Wine Route Loop
name_original: Monturque y Montilla — circular desde Aguilar de la Frontera
description: This moderate 32.8-mile road cycling route from Aguilar de la Frontera connects Monturque and Montilla, winding through vineyards and olive
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aguilar De La Frontera traverse a landscape characterized by extensive olive groves and vineyards, typical of the Cordoban countryside. The region features Vías Verdes, such as the Vía Verde del Aceite, which offer smooth surfaces and gentle slopes on former railway lines. Varied topography includes both flat agricultural plains and hilly terrain, providing panoramic views. Natural reserves like Laguna de Zóñar add ecological interest to the cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Aguilar De La Frontera The most popular road cycling route is Montilla-Moriles Wine Route Loop, a 32.7 miles (52.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route explores the renowned wine-growing region, passing through vineyards and agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Plaza de San José – Pilar de la Dehesa loop from Aguilar de la Frontera, a moderate 31.2 miles (50.2 km) path. This route offers a journey through the local countryside, featuring rolling hills and open fields. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo del Moral – Plaza de San José loop from Aparcamiento del Centro de Visitantes Laguna de Zóñar, a 48.4 miles (77.9 km) trail leading through areas near the Laguna de Zóñar, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Aguilar De La Frontera is defined by extensive olive groves, vineyards, and dedicated Vías Verdes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle greenways to more challenging routes with elevation. The routes in Aguilar De La Frontera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aguilar De La Frontera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Aguilar De La Frontera?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Aguilar De La Frontera, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Aguilar De La Frontera?
A: The terrain around Aguilar De La Frontera is varied. You'll find extensive olive groves and vineyards, characteristic of the Cordoban countryside. The region also features dedicated Vías Verdes, which are former railway lines offering smooth surfaces and generally gentle slopes. Additionally, there are routes with rolling hills and more challenging climbs that provide panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Aguilar De La Frontera?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. The Vías Verdes, such as the Vía Verde del Aceite and the Subbética Greenway Natural Trail, are excellent choices as they offer smooth surfaces and gentle gradients, making them accessible for a wide range of cyclists. These routes are converted from old railway lines, ensuring a tranquil ride away from motor vehicles.

Q: What are some longer road cycling routes near Aguilar De La Frontera?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327661" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San José – Cabra loop from Aparcamiento del Centro de Visitantes Laguna de Zóñar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 79.4 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Moral – Plaza de San José loop from Aparcamiento del Centro de Visitantes Laguna de Zóñar&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 77.9 km long. These routes offer extensive exploration of the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Yes, the broader Andalusian region, including areas around Aguilar De La Frontera, is known for its challenging climbs and scenic roads. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327661" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San José – Cabra loop from Aparcamiento del Centro de Visitantes Laguna de Zóñar&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gain, making them suitable for experienced cyclists seeking a more demanding ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the region's natural beauty. You can cycle through extensive olive groves and vineyards, and some routes pass near significant ecological sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Moral – Plaza de San José loop&lt;/a&gt; leads through areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Zóñar Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt;, a crucial ecological site for birdwatching. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3816980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rincón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Aguilar De La Frontera are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010638895/montilla-moriles-wine-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla-Moriles Wine Route Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20120278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San José – Pilar de la Dehesa loop from Aguilar de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aguilar De La Frontera?
A: The favorable weather conditions in Andalusia, with approximately 320 sunny days a year, make it suitable for cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn are particularly recommended for road cycling in Aguilar De La Frontera, offering pleasant temperatures for longer rides.

Q: Are the Vías Verdes suitable for road bikes, or are they multi-use trails?
A: The Vías Verdes, such as the Vía Verde del Aceite and the Subbética Greenway, are converted from old railway lines and feature smooth surfaces. While they are multi-use trails, their well-maintained condition and gentle slopes make them generally suitable for road bikes, offering a pleasant and traffic-free cycling experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: For families, the Vías Verdes are an excellent choice due to their smooth, traffic-free surfaces and gentle gradients. These routes provide a safe and enjoyable environment for cyclists of all ages, winding through picturesque olive groves and vineyards.

Q: What amenities are available for cyclists, such as cafes or accommodation?
A: Along the Vías Verdes, old train stations have often been repurposed into restaurants and cafes, providing convenient stops for refreshments. In the towns and villages throughout the region, you can find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options that cater to visitors, though specific bike-friendly facilities may vary.

Q: Is public transport available to Aguilar De La Frontera for cyclists?
A: Aguilar De La Frontera is accessible by public transport. While specific policies for carrying bicycles on buses or trains can vary, it's advisable to check with local transport providers in advance regarding their regulations for bicycles, especially for longer journeys.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car if I want to cycle in Aguilar De La Frontera?
A: Parking facilities are generally available in and around Aguilar De La Frontera. For routes that start near specific attractions, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Zóñar Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt;, you may find dedicated parking areas. In the town itself, public parking options are typically available.
tips: Located below the Monturque municipal cemetery and discovered in 1885 during the expansion works. They are the largest Roman cisterns in Spain and the fourth in the world. Declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1996. It is a hydraulic construction from Roman times that was used to store rainwater, made up of twelve chambers covered with a half-barrel vault. The Great Cistern is a work of great monumentality and significance.

Birthplace of Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba, the Great Captain, born in 1453 in the castle of Montilla. City famous for the quality of its Pedro Ximénez grape, integrating the region with the Montilla-Moriles Designation of Origin. Passing through the oldest part of Montilla, it is worth visiting the 16th century church of Santiago.

Work of the architect Juan Vicente Gutiérrez de Salamanca is an octagonal square. Built in 1806 in the neoclassical style, declared a Historic-Artistic Site in May 1974. It is one of the few squares of this type in Spain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there isn't a single designated parking area for this specific circular route, Aguilar de la Frontera, as the starting point, offers various public parking options within the town. Look for street parking or municipal lots that are typically available in Spanish towns.

faqs.question: How challenging is this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. The Montilla-Moriles region is known for its undulating chalky hills, meaning you should anticipate rolling hills and climbs. A moderate level of fitness is beneficial to comfortably enjoy the ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of rural roads, mostly paved, winding through picturesque landscapes. You'll be cycling through vast expanses of vineyards and olive groves, characteristic of the Andalusian countryside. Expect green vineyards contrasting with silver-grey olive trees, traditional wineries, and charming white villages.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. In Monturque, you can visit the Roman cisterns of Monturque. The route also passes through the town of Montilla, known for its wine. Another highlight is the Plaza de San José.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montilla-Moriles region is pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are generally mild, but check local weather forecasts for rain.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route uses public roads and trails, there are no specific restrictions against dogs. However, given it's a road cycling route, dogs would need to be able to keep pace or be carried safely. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and consider local leash laws, especially when passing through towns or near livestock.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and trails within the Montilla-Moriles region.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Paraje Cerro del Humo and the Veredas de Cárdenas.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for food and drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The Montilla-Moriles region is famous for its gastronomy and wine. Both Monturque and Montilla offer cafes, restaurants, and bodegas where you can enjoy local cuisine and taste renowned Montilla-Moriles wines like Fino and Pedro Ximénez.

faqs.question: What makes this cycling route unique?
faqs.answer: This route offers a unique blend of active tourism with the rich wine tradition and cultural heritage of Montilla-Moriles. You get to experience the beautiful Andalusian countryside, visit historic towns, and have the opportunity to culminate your ride with wine tastings and local gastronomy.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 52.7 km racebike route is approximately 2 hours and 32 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.
